children.text: the costs of capital projects to protect, repair, reconstruct, or replace equipment and facilities of the United States maritime transportation system that the Maritime Administrator determines is in danger of suffering serious physical damage, or has suffered serious physical damage, as a result of an emergency; and

children.label: A
children.text: the one-year period beginning on the date of a declaration of an emergency referred to in subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (j)(4); and

children.label: B
children.text: an additional one-year period beginning one year after the date of an emergency referred to in subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (j)(4), if the Maritime Administrator, in consultation with the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, determines there is a compelling need arising out of the emergency for which the declaration is made.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Maritime Administrator shall determine an appropriate method for the equitable allocation and distribution of funds under this section to eligible State and Tribal entities and eligible entities.

children.heading: .—Priority
children.label: 2 .—Priority
children.text: To the extent practicable, in allocating and distributing funds under this section, the Maritime Administrator shall give priority to applications submitted by eligible State or Tribal entities.

children.heading: .—Applications
children.label: c .—Applications
children.text: An applicant for assistance under this section shall submit an application for such assistance to the Maritime Administrator at such time, in such manner, and containing such information and assurances as the Maritime Administrator may require.

children.heading: .—Use of funds
children.label: 1 .—Use of funds
children.text: Funds appropriated to carry out this section shall be in addition to any other funds available under this chapter.

children.heading: .—No effect on other government activity
children.label: 2 .—No effect on other government activity
children.text: The provision of funds under this section shall not affect the ability of any other agency of the Government, including the Federal Emergency Management Agency, or a State agency, a local governmental entity, organization, or person, to provide any other funds otherwise authorized by law.
subject to the terms and conditions the Maritime Administrator determines are necessary;

made only for expenses that are not reimbursed under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.) or any Federal, State, or local assistance program; and42 U.S.C. 5121
made only for expenses that are not reimbursed under any type of marine insurance.

children.heading: .—Federal Share of Costs
children.label: f .—Federal Share of Costs
children.text: The Federal share payable of the costs for which a grant is made under this section shall be 100 percent.

children.heading: .—Administrative Costs
children.label: g .—Administrative Costs
children.text: Of the amounts available to carry out this section, not more than two percent may be used for administration of this section.

children.heading: .—Quality Assurance
children.label: h .—Quality Assurance
children.text: The Maritime Administrator shall institute adequate policies, procedures, and internal controls to prevent waste, fraud, abuse, and program mismanagement for the distribution of funds under this section.
affected the United States maritime transportation system;
mitigated the financial impact of the emergency on the recipient of the assistance; and
protected critical infrastructure in the United States.

children.text: vessel construction, transportation by water, or support activities for transportation by water with an assigned North American Industry Classification System code beginning with 3366, 483, 4883, or 6113, or in the case of such construction, transportation, or support activities conducted by a fish processing vessel, such an assigned code beginning with 3117; or

children.label: i
